PixiJS V5 Tutorial (5) - Texture: Getting Images onto the Canvas

If we can add text, we can add images too. PIXI uses the GPU and WebGL to render images, but an image has to be processed before you can actually use it — and a processed image is usually called a Texture. This post walks through loading images with PIXI.Texture and PIXI.Loader.
Is the Fetch API ES6 or a Web API? Let's Settle This

The Fetch API is one of those things a lot of people misunderstand. The biggest mix-up? People can't tell whether it belongs to ECMAScript 6 or to the Web APIs. So in this post let's dig into where Fetch actually comes from, why everyone assumes it's ES6, and how you can tell the two apart for good.
JavaScript Core Concepts (1): How JavaScript Actually Runs

This is the first post in a series on JavaScript core concepts. We kick things off with how JavaScript actually runs under the hood — what an interpreted language is, how it differs from a compiled one, and what the interpreter does step by step: tokenizing, building the AST, and finally generating code.
JavaScript Core Concepts: The Full Series Index (and Why I Wrote It)

This is the table of contents and intro for my JavaScript Core Concepts series, which I wrote while going through Hexschool's online video course. I'd already gone through the weird parts of JavaScript before, but every instructor explains things their own way — so I figured I'd take notes and learn it again from scratch. This page links to every article in the series.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (4) - Text: Fonts, Strokes, Shadows and Wrapping

Last time we covered Container. This time we're digging into the syntax we already used back then — `new PIXI.Text()`. We'll look at how to set the font, size, color, add a stroke and a drop shadow, wrap long lines, and a cleaner way to manage all of it with TextStyle.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (3): Getting to Grips with Container

We've covered enough of the basics, so now it's time to look at one of the most important pieces of PixiJS — the Container. If you've ever used Bootstrap's grid system, the name will feel familiar, but Pixi's Container works a little differently. This post walks through adding children, reading them back, and dropping some text inside a container.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (2): What WebGL Actually Is, and How the Renderer Works

This one fills in the gaps I skipped last time — what WebGL actually is, why PixiJS leans on your GPU, and how the Renderer works. I'll also show you two ways to check whether your browser is running on WebGL or falling back to plain Canvas.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (1) — Getting Your First Canvas on Screen

PixiJS is a framework built on top of a JavaScript-based 2D engine, and it shows up a lot in 2D web game development. In this first post of the series we'll do a quick getting-started run: how to pull in Pixi, how to create a PIXI.Application, how to tweak the canvas size and background, and how to check which version and render engine Pixi is actually using.
PixiJS V5 Tutorial (0) - Why I'm Doing This + Full Table of Contents

Why write a whole PixiJS V5 tutorial series? This is the opening post for the HexSchool Year-of-the-Rat marathon challenge. I'll talk about how I stumbled into PixiJS, the kinds of situations it's good for, and which chapters and topics this series is going to cover.
if vs switch in JavaScript: Which One Is Actually Faster?

Should you reach for if or switch? It's one of those debates that never really dies. So let's actually test it — comparing the two side by side and looking at the real performance difference, instead of just guessing.
